summary: Frontend testing for Next.js migration - Cart functionality and shared Header/Footer with dark theme. Cart badge updates correctly when adding items. Product page text (Size, Quantity, Total) is clearly readable. However, found critical routing issues where /cart and /faq pages are being served by old React app instead of Next.js app.

ui_bugs.issue: Page shows blank white screen - being served by old React app (id='root') instead of Next.js app. Console shows 'No routes matched location /cart'
Page missing Header component - being served by old React app instead of Next.js app. Has dark content but no shared Header/Footer

integration_issues.issue: Some routes (/cart, /faq) are being served by old React frontend (id='root') instead of Next.js frontend. This is a deployment/routing configuration issue.

Cart Functionality.details: Adding items to cart updates header cart badge count correctly. Toast notification shows 'Kesar Chandan (50g) added to cart!'. Cart badge shows '1' after adding item.

Product Page Text Readability.details: SELECT SIZE label is visible (white text), QUANTITY label is visible (white text), Total: ₹110 is visible (gold text). All text is clearly readable on dark background.

Homepage Header/Footer.details: Header with navigation (Fragrances, Our Story, CSR, Find Retailers, Track Order, Contact) and cart icon present. Dark theme applied.

Our Story Page (/our-story).details: Has shared Header and Footer with dark theme background

Wishlist Page (/wishlist).details: Has shared Header and Footer with dark theme background

Find Retailers Page (/find-retailers).details: Has shared Header and Footer with dark theme background. Shows map and retailer listings.

Track Order Page (/track-order).details: Has shared Header and Footer with dark theme background. Order tracking form visible.

Blog Page (/blog).details: Has shared Header and Footer with dark theme background

Privacy Policy Page (/privacy-policy).details: Has shared Header and Footer with dark theme background

Terms of Service Page (/terms-of-service.details: Has shared Header and Footer with dark theme background

Cart Page (/cart).details: Shows blank white page - being served by old React app instead of Next.js

FAQ Page (/faq).details: Missing Header - being served by old React app instead of Next.js

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix routing configuration so /cart route is served by Next.js app instead of old React app
HIGH: Fix routing configuration so /faq route is served by Next.js app instead of old React app
Check deployment/nginx/ingress configuration to ensure all routes are properly directed to Next.js frontend
critical_code_review_comments: ✓ CartContext.js correctly supports both addToCart signatures
✓ Header.js correctly shows cart badge with count from getCartCount()
✓ ProductActions.js correctly calls addToCart with product, size, quantity
✓ Cart page code (/app/frontend-next/app/cart/page.js) imports Header and Footer correctly
✓ FAQ page code (/app/frontend-next/app/faq/page.js) imports Header and Footer correctly
✗ Routing issue: /cart and /faq routes are being served by old React app (id='root') instead of Next.js app
test_credentials: N/A - no auth required for tested pages
context_for_next_testing_agent: Next.js migration testing found routing issues. The /cart and /faq pages are being served by the old React frontend (identified by id='root' in DOM) instead of the Next.js frontend. Console shows 'No routes matched location /cart'. This is likely a deployment/nginx/ingress configuration issue where certain routes are not properly directed to the Next.js app. All other tested pages (our-story, wishlist, find-retailers, track-order, blog, privacy-policy, terms-of-service) work correctly with shared Header/Footer and dark theme. Cart functionality (adding items, badge update) works correctly on product pages.
rca_of_the_issue: Root Cause: The preview deployment is serving some routes (/cart, /faq) from the old React frontend instead of the Next.js frontend. Evidence: 1) DOM structure shows id='root' which is React app structure, not Next.js. 2) Console logs show 'No routes matched location /cart' from React Router. 3) The Next.js cart page code exists and is correct but is not being served. Fix: Check nginx/ingress configuration to ensure all routes are directed to the Next.js frontend (port 3001) instead of the old React frontend.
